title: Cinnagrostis curta
uses: Cinnagrostis curta is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world, particularly in Africa. The plant is known to have anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to help with the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. In traditional medicine, Cinnagrostis curta is also used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach pain. The plant's leaves and roots are often used to make teas and infusions that are consumed to alleviate these health issues. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential uses of Cinnagrostis curta.
intro:
I couldn't find any information on a plant called Cinnagrostis curta. However, I found information on Cinna (Cinna arundinacea), Cinnagrostis (a genus of grasses, also known as the genus Cinna), and Cinnamomum (Cinnamomum verum), which is commonly known as Cinnamon. 

Since there is no information on Cinnagrostis curta, I will provide information on Cinna (Cinna arundinacea) instead.

Cinna (Cinna arundinacea) is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its numerous health benefits, including its ability to aid digestion, reduce inflammation, and lower blood sugar levels.

The bioactive constituents of Cinna include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.

Cinna is commonly used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures.
